title: Tier A3: Port executing-plans

title: Tier A2: Port subagent-driven-development
description: Replace TodoWrite-driven dispatch with bd ready / bd update --claim. Subagent reads task brief via bd show <id>. Reviewer subagent calls bd close. State now survives compaction and session end.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tier A1: Port using-superpowers
description: Rewrite using-superpowers/SKILL.md so the flowchart node 'Create TodoWrite todo per item' becomes 'bd create per checklist item'. Update the red-flags table to drop TodoWrite references. This is the highest priority because it's the source of the policy conflict that propagates to every session.
close_reason: Closed

title: EPIC: Beads-native rewrite of superpowers skills
description: Port the 14 superpowers SKILL.md files to use bd as the persistence layer instead of TodoWrite and markdown plan files. See plan in conversation history (Tiers A-D) and README.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Verification: all child beads are closed; scripts/preflight.sh -> exit 0 after final skill port; remaining ready list contained only this epic.

title: Design Codex plugin publishing and installation path
description:
Existing agnostic-plugin work covers making the skills themselves Codex-friendly. This issue is specifically for deciding how Codex users discover, install, update, and trust the plugin once those skills are compatible.

Research Codex's current plugin and skill installation mechanisms, then propose a publishing model. Compare at least: GitHub release assets, a marketplace-style index, direct git/repo install, and generated artifacts from shared source. Include how versions, checksums or provenance, docs, and upgrade instructions work for Codex consumers.

The plan should account for both maintainers publishing official artifacts and contributors or users installing from a fork or local checkout.
acceptance_criteria: A concrete Codex publishing/install plan is documented; the plan is distinct from skill content compatibility work; it covers discovery, install, update, versioning, and artifact trust; it supports official releases plus fork/local checkout use; follow-up implementation beads are filed if the chosen plan requires build or release automation.

title: Handle sessions when beads is not installed
description:
When a session starts and the beads CLI is not installed, the plugin should fail gracefully and make the next step explicit without assuming beads is mandatory for this repo or that the user can install tools globally.

The flow should detect missing bd separately from repo initialization problems. It should support different user roles and permission levels: a maintainer who wants to adopt beads, a contributor in someone else's repo, or a user who simply wants to use the skills without beads for this session.

Likely outcomes include: continue without beads for the session, show install options appropriate to the platform, use a repo-local or temporary install path where feasible, or disable beads-specific workflows with clear messaging.
acceptance_criteria: Missing bd is detected before any beads command is required; the user can continue without beads for that session; install guidance avoids assuming global install permissions or maintainer status; repo initialization and CLI installation are handled as separate states; behavior is documented and covered by tests or scripted checks.

title: Handle sessions in repos without beads initialized
description:
When the plugin starts in a repository that does not have beads initialized, it should handle that state intentionally instead of assuming beads is available or that the user wants to initialize it.

The flow should distinguish between: no beads metadata in this repo, beads metadata exists but is invalid, and user is outside a repo. It should explain options without assuming the user is a maintainer with permission to add project files, and without assuming a contributor wants to introduce beads to that repo.

Likely outcomes include a neutral prompt or documented decision path for: continue without beads for this session, initialize beads locally or in the repo when appropriate, or point at existing project tracking if beads is not desired.
acceptance_criteria: Starting a session in a repo with no beads metadata produces a clear non-fatal path; users can continue without beads for that session; initialization is never automatic without explicit user intent; messaging does not assume maintainer privileges; contributor-safe behavior is documented and covered by tests or scripted checks.

title: Consolidate AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md to a single source of truth
description:
AGENTS.md (read by Codex and other agnostic harnesses) and CLAUDE.md (read by Claude) duplicate content and have drifted apart:
- AGENTS.md has a non-interactive shell rules section that CLAUDE.md does not.
- CLAUDE.md has the repo-layout / conventions / preflight section that AGENTS.md does not.
- Both contain a beads-integration block, but only AGENTS.md's is auto-managed.

Result: Codex sees layout-blind instructions, Claude sees shell-rule-blind instructions. Inconsistent guidance defeats both files.

Pick one source of truth (probably AGENTS.md, since superpowers-beads-zr6 wants the plugin agnostic). Make the other file a thin pointer or symlink. Keep the auto-managed beads block intact in whichever file beads writes to.
acceptance_criteria: Layout, conventions, preflight, beads rules, and shell-safety rules all appear once and are reachable from both Claude and Codex sessions.
notes: Tightly coupled to superpowers-beads-zr6 (Make plugin Claude/Codex agnostic). Likely should be done as part of that effort, but file separately so it is not lost.

title: Run scripts/preflight.sh in CI
description:
.github/workflows/validate.yml only runs 'claude plugin validate .'. The canonical gate per docs/preflight.md and CLAUDE.md is scripts/preflight.sh, which additionally runs git diff --check, bd orphans, bd stale, and the in-progress check. Today PRs can land with stale/orphaned beads and unresolved in-progress issues.

This is a subset of the broader CI work in superpowers-beads-fxd, but is small and unblocked: just install bd in CI and run preflight.sh. Do this before fxd to stop further drift.
acceptance_criteria: CI installs bd, runs scripts/preflight.sh on PR + main, and fails the workflow on a non-zero exit.
notes: Depends on a CI-friendly bd install path. If bd has no published binary release, decide whether to skip the bd-dependent checks in CI or build bd from source.

title: Reconcile Task tool (superpowers:code-reviewer) usage with no registered code-reviewer agent
description:
plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/subagent-driven-development/code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md and SKILL.md (and writing-plans/plan-issue-reviewer-prompt.md, brainstorming/epic-reviewer-prompt.md, the other subagent-driven-development reviewer prompts) all instruct the controller to dispatch a named agent like 'superpowers:code-reviewer'. plugin.json does not register any agents — there is no 'agents' field and no agents/ directory. On Claude the named-agent dispatch will fail; on Codex it cannot work at all (see references/codex-tools.md).

Either:
- Register an actual code-reviewer agent in the plugin (add agents/ directory + manifest entry once Claude/Codex schemas are known), or
- Rewrite the prompts to dispatch a generic agent with the inline prompt content from code-reviewer.md.

This overlaps with the agnostic-plugin work (superpowers-beads-zr6), but the fix is needed regardless of harness because the current state is broken on Claude too.
acceptance_criteria: Either an agents/ entry exists for code-reviewer (and any other named agents the prompts reference), or the prompts dispatch generic agents with inline content. No skill instructs the controller to call a named agent that the plugin does not register.

title: Fix broken cross-skill template reference in subagent-driven-development
description: plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/subagent-driven-development/code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md:11 instructs the controller to 'Use template at requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md', but the relative path is wrong from this skill's directory — the file lives at ../requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md. The reference will fail wherever an agent resolves it as written. Fix the path, or change to a stable cross-skill reference form (e.g., superpowers:requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md) once an agnostic format is decided.
acceptance_criteria: The reference resolves from the skill's directory, OR the skill no longer points at another skill's file.
close_reason: Fixed in small-fixes-bundle branch; claude plugin validate passes.

title: Thorough code review of current repo state
description:
Do a thorough code review of the entire superpowers-beads repo as it stands today. Goal: surface bugs, inconsistencies, drift from conventions, security issues, and opportunities to simplify before the harness-agnostic restructure (zr6) lands.

Scope:
- Marketplace + plugin manifests (.claude-plugin/marketplace.json, plugins/superpowers-beads/.claude-plugin/plugin.json) — schema correctness, version sync, source paths.
- Every SKILL.md — frontmatter completeness, internal consistency, dead links, attribution to obra/superpowers where applicable, accuracy of any commands/snippets they instruct the agent to run.
- Workflow formulas (any superpowers workflow / formula files).
- scripts/preflight.sh and docs/preflight.md — does the script match what the doc claims, does it actually catch what it should.
- Top-level docs (README.md, CLAUDE.md) — accuracy vs. current code.
- Repo hygiene: stray files (.codex was untracked at session start), gitignore correctness, license headers.

Output: a structured review report (findings grouped by severity) filed back into beads as follow-up issues for anything actionable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every file in the repo has been read at least once during the review.
- Findings are categorized (bug / inconsistency / convention drift / nit) with file:line references.
- Each actionable finding becomes its own bead, linked back to this review issue.
- A short summary is posted in this issue's notes.
notes:
Review complete. 10 follow-up beads filed:

Bug:
- superpowers-beads-dsl (P2) — Fix broken cross-skill template reference in subagent-driven-development
- superpowers-beads-tnv (P2) — Reconcile Task tool (superpowers:code-reviewer) usage with no registered code-reviewer agent
- superpowers-beads-7o7 (P3) — Harden in-progress detection in scripts/preflight.sh

Task (CI/process):
- superpowers-beads-13i (P2) — Run scripts/preflight.sh in CI [now blocks fxd]
- superpowers-beads-bl0 (P2) — Consolidate AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md [blocked by zr6]

Task (docs/cleanup):
- superpowers-beads-95m (P3) — Refresh README Status section
- superpowers-beads-ndy (P3) — Fix brainstorming description and Gemini auto-load claim
- superpowers-beads-s1v (P4) — Replace stale issue ID example in docs/preflight.md
- superpowers-beads-nc5 (P4) — Decide on .codex root file
- superpowers-beads-0rd (P4) — Parameterize systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh

No code changes from this review pass — review-only scope.

Verification: `claude plugin validate .` -> exit 0 (validation passed) confirms manifest health at the time of review.
close_reason: Thorough code review complete; 10 follow-up beads filed with file:line references. Plugin validate passed at time of review.

title: Build full CI workflow with dual Claude/Codex publishing
description:
The repo has scripts/preflight.sh but no CI. We need a proper CI workflow that runs on every PR and on main, plus a publish pipeline that releases the plugin for both Claude Code and Codex consumers.

Scope:
- CI (PRs + main):
  - Run scripts/preflight.sh (and bd preflight where appropriate).
  - Validate marketplace.json / plugin.json schemas.
  - Validate skill frontmatter (name, description present; lint conventions).
  - Verify version bump consistency between marketplace.json and plugin.json.
  - Run any unit/integration checks we add.
- Release/publish:
  - Trigger on tag or release.
  - Produce + publish a Claude marketplace artifact (current shape).
  - Produce + publish a Codex-compatible artifact (shape TBD by the agnostic-plugin work).
  - Generate release notes / changelog.
- Caching, matrix (if needed for multiple harness validators), and concurrency guards.

Out of scope: the actual harness-agnostic restructure (separate bead). This issue assumes that work has produced a build target per harness and wires CI around it.
design:
Likely GitHub Actions. Decisions to make:
- Where do Codex artifacts get published (registry, GH release asset, separate repo)? Mirrors the Claude marketplace question.
- Do we sign/verify artifacts?
- How are secrets scoped for the publish job?
acceptance_criteria:
- PRs are gated on a green CI run that exercises preflight + schema/frontmatter validation + version-bump check.
- main is protected and runs the same gates.
- A tagged release produces and publishes artifacts for both Claude and Codex consumers.
- Failed publishes do not leave half-released state (atomic or clearly recoverable).

title: Make plugin Claude/Codex agnostic
description:
The plugin is currently structured solely for Claude Code (.claude-plugin/, plugin.json, marketplace.json, SKILL.md frontmatter conventions). We want it to be usable from both Claude Code and Codex so the same skill content can be consumed by either harness.

Scope:
- Audit current Claude-specific assumptions (directory layout, manifest schemas, frontmatter fields, skill invocation conventions, hooks/settings references).
- Research Codex's equivalent plugin/skill packaging format and identify the delta vs. Claude.
- Decide on a structure: shared source-of-truth for skill bodies + harness-specific manifests, or a build step that emits per-harness artifacts, or dual-format files where viable.
- Update README/CLAUDE.md to describe the dual-target model.
- Migrate existing skills (test-driven-development, using-git-worktroots, writing-skills, dispatching-parallel-agents, superpowers workflow formulas) to the new structure without regressing Claude behavior.

Out of scope: the actual CI/publish pipeline (tracked separately).
design:
Open questions to resolve during design:
- Is there a single manifest format both harnesses accept, or do we need per-harness manifests generated from a shared source?
- Where do Claude-specific concepts (hooks, slash commands, settings.json) map in Codex, and what happens to skills that rely on them?
- Should the repo rename from 'superpowers-beads' (Claude-marketplace flavored) to a harness-neutral name, or keep the name and just broaden scope?
acceptance_criteria:
- Skills load and run correctly under both Claude Code and Codex from a single checkout.
- No skill is silently Claude-only without an explicit, documented opt-out.
- README documents how to install/use the plugin from each harness.
- Existing Claude users see no regression.

title: Patch pre-commit hook for missing timeout binary
description: Beads' .beads/hooks/pre-commit looks for the 'timeout' binary but macOS doesn't ship it (coreutils provides 'gtimeout'). When timeout is missing, the hook silently runs without a timeout and can deadlock. Patch the hook to also check for gtimeout, or fall through to a perl-alarm shim. This bit us during bd init in this repo. Consider upstreaming to beads as well.
close_reason: Patched pre-commit hook to use timeout, gtimeout, then a Perl alarm shim before falling back without timeout.

title: Fix brainstorming SKILL description and Gemini auto-load claim
description:
Two small inconsistencies in skill metadata/description:

1. plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md frontmatter description starts with 'You MUST use this...'. Every other skill (and the writing-skills convention itself, SKILL.md:96) starts with 'Use when...'. Bring brainstorming in line.

2. plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:41 claims 'Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md'. There is no GEMINI.md shipped by this plugin, so the auto-load claim is misleading. Either ship one or rephrase to say users must reference references/gemini-tools.md themselves.
acceptance_criteria: Brainstorming description starts with 'Use when'. The Gemini claim is either accurate (GEMINI.md exists) or rephrased to match reality.

The tier system is not referenced in any skill or doc. Either update the status to reflect current state (skills ported, listing them) or drop the section in favor of the bead tracker as source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: README accurately reflects what is in main today.
notes: Trivial; bundle with the next docs PR.

title: Harden in-progress detection in scripts/preflight.sh
description:
scripts/preflight.sh has two fragility issues in its in-progress check:

1. Line 18: 'in_progress_count=$(... | grep -c "\"id\":")' counts JSON 'id' keys with a regex grep. This will overcount if an id-shaped string appears inside another field's value, and undercount on minified JSON.
2. Line 24: 'grep -q "\"id\": \"$allowed_in_progress\""' interprets the variable as a regex. The example documented in docs/preflight.md (superpowers-beads-isj.1) contains a literal '.' that grep treats as 'any character'.

Fix by using jq (preferred) or grep -F for fixed-string matching, and by counting via 'jq length' instead of grep -c.
acceptance_criteria: Preflight correctly counts in-progress issues from arbitrary bd JSON output, and the ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS match is exact-string.
close_reason: Replaced grep-based JSON count with 'jq length' and switched the allow-list match to a fixed-string jq existence check. Verified: ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS=superpowers-beads-7o7 ./scripts/preflight.sh -> 'Plugin preflight passed.'

description: Prefer bd worktree over manual git worktree. Auto-claim the underlying issue on creation.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts

Port adds using-git-worktrees with bd worktree create/list/remove and issue claim-first flow, and updates branch cleanup guidance to bd worktree.

title: Parameterize systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh test runner
description:
plugins/superpowers-beads/skills/systematic-debugging/find-polluter.sh:42 hardcodes 'npm test "$TEST_FILE"'. The script ships in a general-purpose plugin and may run in non-Node projects. Either:
- Add a TEST_CMD environment variable defaulting to 'npm test' so users in pytest/go/cargo projects can override.
- Document that the script is Node-specific in its header.
acceptance_criteria: Either the script is parameterized via env var, or the limitation is documented in the script header and in the SKILL that references it.

title: Decide on .codex root file (remove or document)
description:
A 0-byte read-only file '.codex' exists at the repo root. It is untracked, not in .gitignore, and not referenced in any doc, manifest, or script. Likely an artifact from local Codex experimentation. Two options:

1. Delete it and add '.codex' to .gitignore if Codex creates such markers locally.
2. If it is intentionally a marker for the agnostic-plugin work (zr6), document why and either track it or .gitignore it.

Decide and act so 'git status' is clean.
acceptance_criteria: The repo root has no untracked '.codex' surprise — either deleted, ignored, or tracked-and-explained.

title: Replace stale issue ID example in docs/preflight.md
description: docs/preflight.md:20 references 'superpowers-beads-isj.1' as the ALLOW_IN_PROGRESS example. That issue and its sub-issue are closed; the literal id no longer means anything to a reader. Replace with a generic placeholder like '<bead-implementing-preflight>' or '$ACTIVE_BEAD'.
acceptance_criteria: No stale concrete issue IDs remain in docs/preflight.md.
close_reason: Fixed in small-fixes-bundle branch; claude plugin validate passes.
